title: AI in the Classroom: A Revolution in Learning?
passage:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly transforming various sectors, and education is no exception.  The integration of AI in educational settings promises a more personalized, efficient, and engaging learning experience for students.  One of the most significant applications of AI in education is adaptive learning platforms. These systems use algorithms to analyze a student's performance, identify their strengths and weaknesses, and then tailor the learning content and pace accordingly.  For instance, if a student struggles with a particular mathematical concept, the AI can provide additional explanations, practice problems, or even suggest alternative learning resources. This individualized approach ensures that no student is left behind and that advanced learners are adequately challenged.

Beyond personalized learning, AI can also automate administrative tasks, freeing up educators to focus more on teaching and student interaction. Grading assignments, scheduling classes, and managing student records are all areas where AI can significantly reduce the workload. Furthermore, AI-powered tools can provide valuable insights into student engagement and progress, allowing teachers to intervene proactively when a student appears to be struggling or disengaging.  Virtual tutors and chatbots, for example, can offer instant support and answer common student queries, providing a readily available resource outside of traditional classroom hours.

However, the widespread adoption of AI in education is not without its challenges. Concerns about data privacy and security are paramount, as these systems collect vast amounts of sensitive student information. Ensuring equitable access to AI-powered tools is another crucial issue, as a digital divide could exacerbate existing educational inequalities.  Moreover, the role of the human teacher remains indispensable. While AI can enhance the learning process, it cannot replicate the empathy, critical thinking facilitation, and emotional support that a human educator provides.  The future of AI in education likely lies in a collaborative model, where AI acts as a powerful assistant to teachers, augmenting their capabilities and ultimately enriching the educational journey for all.

A good teacher is indispensable for student development.

vocabulary.example: The new features are augmenting the software's capabilities.

**For Multiple Choice questions:** Identify the core of the question and then scan the passage for information related to it. Eliminate options that are clearly incorrect based on the text.

title: Working from Home: A New Way to Be Productive
passage:
The way people work is changing. Many companies now allow their employees to work from home, also known as remote work. This means people don't have to go to an office every day. They can work from their house, a coffee shop, or anywhere with an internet connection.

Some people think working from home is great. They say they can be more productive. This is because they have fewer distractions. In an office, there are often many noises and interruptions. At home, people can create a quiet space to focus on their tasks. They also save time and money by not commuting. This extra time can be used for work or for personal activities.

However, not everyone finds remote work easy. Some people miss the social interaction of the office. They might feel lonely. It can also be hard to separate work from personal life. When your home is also your office, it's easy to keep working late or to feel like you are always on duty. For some jobs, being in the same room as colleagues is important for collaboration and quick problem-solving.

Despite the challenges, remote work is becoming more common. Companies are finding ways to make it work. They use technology like video calls and online chat to keep teams connected. Many believe that with the right approach, remote work can be a very effective way to get things done.

vocabulary.meaning: working from a location away from the main office, usually home
things that prevent someone from giving their full attention to something
traveling some distance between one's home and place of work on a regular basis
the action of working with someone to produce or create something
